findings_assessment.justification: The JavaScript comment best-practice recommendation ('コメントは保守性を向上させる') is a general style guideline, not a technical constraint. Its absence does not affect correctness of implementation. Implementers follow project conventions regardless, and omitting this advisory note has no bearing on whether the popup window pattern works correctly.

The `attributeName` parameter's default behavior (falls back to `name` attribute when null) is already visible in the included function implementation: `if (attributeName == null) { attributeName = "name"; }`. An implementer reading the code can infer this directly. The missing JSDoc text is redundant given the code is present. The D→E fix agent left this unchanged because the information is already conveyed implicitly through the code.
